incidents.vector: Attackers compromised LayerZero's Decentralized Verifier Network — the sole message verifier for Kelp's rsETH token — then forged bridge messages to mint roughly 116,500 rsETH with no Ether backing, borrowed real assets against the fake collateral, and DDoS'd backup systems to block intervention.
incidents.impact: Roughly $290-300 million stolen, the largest DeFi exploit of 2026. Funds are already being laundered through THORChain, Umbra, and BitTorrent, and downstream lenders such as Aave are scrambling to contain contagion as users rush to withdraw.

incidents.vector: Lumma Stealer infostealer on a Context.ai employee (seeded by a Roblox cheat download) exfiltrated OAuth tokens for Context.ai's AI Office Suite, including a token tied to a Vercel employee's Google Workspace account that had been granted 'Allow All' permissions.
incidents.impact: Attackers pivoted into Vercel's AWS environment and read environment variables for a limited set of customers, who were told to rotate credentials. A group claiming to be ShinyHunters is offering the stolen data for $2 million. Mandiant and CrowdStrike are leading the response; the blast radius across Context.ai's other OAuth customers is still being scoped.

incidents.victim: MSPs and their downstream customers (via Bomgar/BeyondTrust Remote Support)
incidents.vector: Unauthenticated RCE flaw CVE-2026-1731 in Bomgar/BeyondTrust Remote Support servers, chained with the leaked LockBit 3.0 builder and RMM tools (AnyDesk, Atera) for persistence and lateral movement.
incidents.impact: Huntress observed at least five separate intrusions in two weeks. One April 15 MSP compromise led to mass isolation of 78 downstream businesses and confirmed exploitation at four of them; an April 3 dental-software vendor compromise pivoted into three customer networks. Multiple incidents ended in LockBit ransomware deployment.

incidents.vector: A group calling itself TeamPCP used stolen maintainer credentials to gain write access to the LiteLLM Python package (~97M monthly downloads) and to Checkmarx's GitHub Actions workflows, injecting a credential-harvesting payload that silently exfiltrated cloud keys, secrets, and the AI API keys LiteLLM was designed to manage.
incidents.impact: Cascading compromise across five software distribution ecosystems in a week, tied to an earlier compromise of Aqua Security's Trivy. CISA added the campaign to KEV. The group references ransomware victims and has threatened to release over 300 GB of stolen data; possible collaboration with Lapsus$ reported.

incidents.victim: Government agencies, law enforcement, email providers (200+ organizations, 5,000+ devices)
incidents.vector: Known vulnerabilities in end-of-life Mikrotik and TP-Link SOHO routers were exploited to modify DNS settings and route traffic through attacker-controlled resolvers, enabling adversary-in-the-middle interception of Microsoft Office OAuth authentication tokens with no malware on the target network.
incidents.impact: At peak in December 2025 the campaign ensnared more than 18,000 routers. Lumen/Black Lotus Labs, Microsoft, NSA, FBI, and UK NCSC published joint advisories; targets included ministries of foreign affairs, law enforcement, and third-party email providers. FBI Operation Masquerade disrupted a large portion of the infrastructure.

incidents.vector: Malicious code injected into a package inside the Axios JavaScript library (roughly 100 million weekly downloads), with payloads built to run across macOS, Windows, and Linux.
incidents.impact: Detected within minutes by Elastic and removed in about three hours, but the package may still have been downloaded more than half a million times in that window, exposing enterprise, startup, and government systems that depend on Axios.

incidents.victim: ANTS (French National Agency for Secure Documents)
incidents.vector: Undisclosed intrusion into the ANTS portal used to process passports, national ID cards, residence permits, and driver's licenses.
incidents.impact: Login credentials, names, email addresses, dates of birth, and account identifiers — and in some cases postal addresses, places of birth, and phone numbers — may have been exposed for individual and professional accounts. Uploaded application documents were not affected. Investigation ongoing.

incidents.vector: Unspecified cyberattack that forced the city to shut down its IT network; surrounding Leon County preemptively disconnected from the city network, language consistent with ransomware containment.
incidents.impact: Several city websites and public services remain unavailable days after the attack. No attribution or ransomware family publicly identified.

incidents.vector: Attackers seeded fake token pools on the platform over several days, then exploited vulnerabilities in the margin-trading feature to route operations through the staged pools.
incidents.impact: $18.4 million in crypto assets drained from the DeFi protocol.

incidents.vector: Sophisticated distributed denial-of-service attack, with follow-on waves throughout the week.
incidents.impact: Roughly a day of intermittent outages across Bluesky; follow-on attacks mitigated. No data compromise reported.

incidents.vector: Purpose-built OT malware (ZionSiphon) designed to locate specific Israeli water/desalination vendor software and rewrite configuration to max water-pump pressure and chlorine dosing; politically motivated strings reference 'support of our brothers in Iran, Palestine, and Yemen' and explicit intent to poison Tel Aviv and Haifa.
incidents.impact: Malware failed to trigger due to a XOR-encoding bug in its IP-range check, causing it to self-delete. Darktrace has not confirmed whether it was deployed in the wild; the sample surfaces the continuing Iran-linked threat to Israeli water utilities.

incidents.vector: Active exploitation of CVE-2026-32201, a SharePoint Server spoofing vulnerability that lets attackers present falsified content or interfaces over the network, enabling phishing and social engineering from trusted SharePoint environments.
incidents.impact: Part of Microsoft's April 2026 Patch Tuesday (167 fixes, the second-largest monthly batch on record). CVE-2026-32201 is confirmed under active attack; a separate Windows Defender privilege-escalation flaw (BlueHammer, CVE-2026-33825) had exploit code leaked before patch. Organizations urged to patch immediately.
